question: What are the tags of the top 5 least downloaded live albums?
evidence: least downloaded album refers to MIN(totalSnatched where releaseType = 'album');
SQL:
WITH least5 AS (
    SELECT `id`
    FROM `torrents`
    WHERE `releaseType` = 'live album'
    ORDER BY `totalSnatched` ASC
    LIMIT 5
)
SELECT DISTINCT `tag`
FROM `tags`
WHERE `id` IN (SELECT `id` FROM least5);

question: What is the tag and the artist of the most downloaded single?
evidence: the most downloaded single refers to MAX(totalSnatched where releaseType = 'single');
SQL:
SELECT t2.`tag`, t1.`artist`
FROM `torrents` AS t1
JOIN `tags` AS t2 ON t1.`id` = t2.`id`
WHERE t1.`releaseType` = 'single'
  AND t1.`totalSnatched` = (
        SELECT MAX(`totalSnatched`)
        FROM `torrents`
        WHERE `releaseType` = 'single'
      );

question: How many releases by the artist michael jackson are tagged "pop"?
evidence: tag = 'pop';
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id) FROM torrents AS T1 INNER JOIN tags AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.id WHERE T2.tag = 'pop' AND T1.artist = 'michael jackson'

question: Name the title of the top three releases with the highest number of downloads.
evidence: title refers to groupName; the highest number of downloads refers to MAX(totalSnatched);
SQL: SELECT groupName FROM torrents ORDER BY totalSnatched DESC LIMIT 3

question: Indicates the titles of releases with ids from 10 to 20 and singles downloaded at least 20.
evidence: releaseType = 'single'; downloaded at least 20 refers to totalSnatched ≥ 20; id from 10 to 20 refer to id between 10 and 20; titles refer to groupName;
SQL: SELECT groupName FROM torrents WHERE totalSnatched >= 20 AND releaseType LIKE 'single' AND id BETWEEN 10 AND 20

question: Provide the name of artists who had no more than 100 downloads and are tagged "funk" in 1980.
evidence: no more than 100 downloads refer to totalSnatched ≤ 100; groupYear = 1980; tag = 'funk';
SQL: SELECT T1.artist FROM torrents AS T1 INNER JOIN tags AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.id WHERE T2.tag = 'funk' AND T1.groupYear = 1980 GROUP BY T1.artist HAVING SUM(T1.totalSnatched) <= 100

question: Which artist has released the most singles with the tag "soul"?
evidence: the most singles refer to MAX(COUNT(releaseType = 'single'));
SQL:
SELECT artist
FROM (
    SELECT t1.artist, COUNT(DISTINCT t1.id) AS cnt
    FROM torrents t1
    JOIN tags t2 ON t1.id = t2.id
    WHERE t2.tag = 'soul'
      AND t1.releaseType = 'single'
    GROUP BY t1.artist
) x
WHERE cnt = (
    SELECT MAX(cnt)
    FROM (
        SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id) AS cnt
        FROM torrents t1
        JOIN tags t2 ON t1.id = t2.id
        WHERE t2.tag = 'soul'
          AND t1.releaseType = 'single'
        GROUP BY t1.artist
    )
);

question: What's the tag genre of the artist with id 16?
SQL: SELECT T2.tag FROM torrents AS T1 INNER JOIN tags AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.id WHERE T1.id = 16

question: Please list all release titles whose tag is jazz in 2005.

SQL: SELECT T1.groupName FROM torrents AS T1 INNER JOIN tags AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.id WHERE T1.groupYear = 2005 AND T2.tag LIKE 'jazz'

question: Among the countries with country notes on the series code SM.POP.TOTL, how many of them are in the low-income group?

SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.Countrycode) FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN CountryNotes AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Countrycode WHERE T2.Seriescode = 'SM.POP.TOTL' AND T1.IncomeGroup = 'Low income' AND T2.Description IS NOT NULL;

question: How many low-income countries under the lending category of the International Development Associations have a country note on the series code SM.POP.TOTL?

question: What is the description of the footnote on the series code AG.LND.FRST.K2 in 1990 for Aruba?
evidence: Year = 1990; Aruba is the name of country where ShortName = 'Aruba'
SQL: SELECT T2.Description FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN FootNotes AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Countrycode WHERE T1.ShortName = 'Aruba' AND T2.Seriescode = 'AG.LND.FRST.K2' AND T2.Year = 'YR1990'

question: On which years did Aruba got a footnote on the series code AG.LND.FRST.K2?
evidence: Aruba is the name of country where ShortName = 'Aruba'

question: What are the special notes for the country whose average adolescent fertility rate is the highest?
evidence: the average adolescent fertility rate is DIVIDE(SUM(value), SUM(IndicatorName like 'adolescent fertility rate%')); MAX(average adolescent fertility rate)
SQL:
WITH country_avg AS (
  SELECT `CountryCode`, AVG(`Value`) AS avg_rate
  FROM `Indicators`
  WHERE `IndicatorName` LIKE 'Adolescent fertility rate%'
  GROUP BY `CountryCode`
), top_country AS (
  SELECT `CountryCode`
  FROM country_avg
  ORDER BY avg_rate DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT SpecialNotes
FROM country_avg
JOIN country USING (`CountryCode`)
WHERE avg_rate = (SELECT MAX(avg_rate) FROM country_avg)

question: In 1970, how many Middle Eastern & North African countries whose value for CO2 emissions from gaseous fuel consumption (kt) indicator is more than 600?
evidence: Year = 1970; Middle East & North Africa is the name of the region where Region = 'Middle East & North Africa'; CO2 emissions from gaseous fuel consumption (kt) is the name of indicator where IndicatorName = 'CO2 emissions from gaseous fuel consumption (kt)'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.CountryCode)  FROM Indicators AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Region = 'Middle East & North Africa' AND T1.IndicatorName = 'CO2 emissions from gaseous fuel consumption (kt)' AND T1.Year = 1970 AND T1.Value > 600

question: Which countries have a fertility rate between 4 and 5 in 1979? List their names.
evidence: Which countries have a fertility rate between 4 and 5 in 1979? List their short names.
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT c.`ShortName`
FROM `Indicators` i
JOIN `Country`  c ON c.`CountryCode` = i.`CountryCode`
WHERE i.`Year` = 1979
  AND i.`IndicatorName` = 'Fertility rate, total (births per woman)'
  AND i.`Value` BETWEEN 4 AND 5
  AND COALESCE(c.`Region`, '') <> '';

question: List the country code of the upper middle income country which has the lowest value of CO2 emissions (kt).
evidence: IncomeGroup = 'Upper middle income'; IndicatorName = 'CO2 emissions (kt); the lowest value refers to MIN(Value);
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT T1.CountryCode 
FROM Country AS T1 
INNER JOIN Indicators AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.CountryCode 
WHERE T1.IncomeGroup = 'Upper middle income' AND T2.IndicatorName = 'CO2 emissions (kt)' 
AND T2.Value = (
  SELECT MIN(T4.Value)
  FROM Country AS T3
  INNER JOIN Indicators AS T4 ON T3.CountryCode=T4.CountryCode
  WHERE T3.IncomeGroup = 'Upper middle income' AND T4.IndicatorName = 'CO2 emissions (kt)' 
  ORDER BY T4.Value ASC LIMIT 1)

question: In 2005, which series codes use the International Monetary Fund, Balance of Payments Statistics Yearbook and data files source?
evidence: Year contains '2005'; Series.Source LIKE 'International Monetary Fund, Balance of Payments Statistics Yearbook and data files%'
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT s.SeriesCode
FROM Series s
JOIN Indicators i ON i.IndicatorCode = s.SeriesCode
WHERE s.Source LIKE 'International Monetary Fund, Balance of Payments Statistics Yearbook and data files%'
  AND i.Year = 2005;

question: From 1968 to 1970, what are indicator names whose license type is open and values are less than 100?
evidence: From 1968 to 1970 refers to Year between '1968' and '1970'; values are less than 100 imply Value<100;
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT i.`IndicatorName`
FROM `Indicators` i
JOIN `Series`  s ON i.`IndicatorCode` = s.`SeriesCode`
WHERE i.`Year` BETWEEN 1968 AND 1970
  AND s.`LicenseType` = 'Open'
  AND i.`Value` < 100;

question: Which country had the highest value of indicator belongs to Private Sector & Trade: Exports topic? Please list the country name and indicator name.
evidence: country refers to CountryName;
SQL:
SELECT T1.CountryName, T1.IndicatorName 
FROM Indicators AS T1 INNER JOIN Series AS T2 
ON T1.IndicatorName = T2.IndicatorName WHERE T2.Topic = 'Private Sector & Trade: Exports' AND T1.CountryName NOT IN ('World', 'High income', 'OECD members', 'High income: OECD', 'Europe & Central Asia (all income levels)', 'European Union', 'East Asia & Pacific (all income levels)', 'Low & middle income', 'Middle income', 'Euro area', 'Upper middle income', 'Upper middle income', 'High income: nonOECD', 'East Asia & Pacific (developing only)')
AND T1.Value = (
  SELECT MAX(T3.Value)
  FROM Indicators AS T3 INNER JOIN Series AS T4 
ON T3.IndicatorName = T4.IndicatorName WHERE T4.Topic = 'Private Sector & Trade: Exports' AND T3.CountryName NOT IN ('World', 'High income', 'OECD members', 'High income: OECD', 'Europe & Central Asia (all income levels)', 'European Union', 'East Asia & Pacific (all income levels)', 'Low & middle income', 'Middle income', 'Euro area', 'Upper middle income', 'Upper middle income', 'High income: nonOECD', 'East Asia & Pacific (developing only)'))

question: Among the low income countries, which country has the lowest fertility rate? List the names of these countries.
evidence: fertility rate refers to IndicatorName = 'Adolescent fertility rate (births per 1,000 women ages 15-19)'; lowest refers to MIN(Value); IncomeGroup = 'Low income';

WITH country_value AS (

  FROM Country AS T1 
  INNER JOIN Indicators AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.CountryCode 
  WHERE T1.IncomeGroup = 'Low income' AND T2.IndicatorName = 'Adolescent fertility rate (births per 1,000 women ages 15-19)'

question: Name the country in which the topic is about Poverty: Shared Prosperity. Indicate the long name of the country.

question: Please list the short names of any 3 countries that use U.S. dollar.

question: Which country has the lowest percentage of arable land ever recorded?

FROM `Indicators`

question: What proportion of Sub-Saharan Africa's countries have lower middle incomes?
evidence: proportion = divide(count(countrycode where incomegroup = 'Low income'), count(countrycode))*100%; Sub-Saharan Africa's countries refers to region = 'Sub-Saharan Africa'; have lower middle incomes refers to incomegroup = 'Low income'
SQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN IncomeGroup = 'Lower middle income' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(CountryCode) persentage FROM Country WHERE Region = 'Sub-Saharan Africa'

question: How many countries have their country's footnotes described as "unspecified"?
evidence: described as "unspecified" refers to Description = 'Not specified';
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.CountryCode)  FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN Footnotes AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Countrycode WHERE T2.Description = 'Not specified'

question: What is the agricultural land area in sq. km of Italy in 1968?
evidence: agricultural land area in sq. km refers value where indicatorname = 'Agricultural land (sq. km)'; Italy refers to countryname = 'Italy'; in 1968 refers to year = '1968'
SQL: SELECT Value FROM Indicators WHERE IndicatorName = 'Agricultural land (sq. km)' AND Year = 1968 AND CountryName = 'Italy'

question: What is the lending category of the country with a cereal production of 6140000 metric tons for the year 1966?
evidence: cereal production of 6140000 metric tons refers value where IndicatorName = 'Cereal production (metric tons)' and Value=6140000; the year 1966 refers to Year = '1966'
SQL: SELECT T1.LendingCategory FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN Indicators AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.IndicatorName = 'Cereal production (metric tons)' AND T2.Value = 6140000 AND T2.Year = 1966

question: What is the average number of passengers carried via air transport per year by Bulgaria between 1970 to 1980? Indicate the country's system of trade.
evidence: average number refers to avg(value); passengers carried via air transport per year refers to value where IndicatorName = 'Air transport, passengers carried'; by Bulgaria refers to CountryName = 'Bulgaria'; between 1970 to 1980 refers to Year between 1970 and 1980
SQL: SELECT AVG(T1.Value), T2.SystemOfTrade FROM Indicators AS T1 INNER JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.IndicatorName = 'Air transport, passengers carried' AND T1.Year >= 1970 AND T1.Year < 1981 AND T1.CountryName = 'Bulgaria'

question: What is the note for Australia on the indicator SP.ADO.TFRT?
evidence: note refers to Description; for Australia refers to ShortName = 'Australia'; indicator SP.ADO.TFRT refers to Seriescode = 'SP.ADO.TFRT'
SQL: SELECT T2.Description FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN CountryNotes AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Countrycode WHERE T2.Seriescode = 'SP.ADO.TFRT' AND T1.ShortName = 'Australia'

question: Please list the country notes for Aruba on the indicators under the topic of Environment: Energy production & use.
evidence: note refers to Description; for Aruba refers to ShortName = 'Aruba'
SQL: SELECT T2.Description FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN CountryNotes AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Countrycode INNER JOIN Series AS T3 ON T2.Seriescode = T3.SeriesCode WHERE T1.ShortName = 'Aruba' AND T3.Topic = 'Environment: Energy production & use'

question: Which countries have country notes on the indicator BX.KLT.DINV.CD.WD? List their short names
evidence: indicator BX.KLT.DINV.CD.WD refers to Seriescode = 'BX.KLT.DINV.CD.WD'
SQL: SELECT T1.ShortName FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN CountryNotes AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Countrycode INNER JOIN Series AS T3 ON T2.Seriescode = T3.SeriesCode WHERE T3.Seriescode = 'BX.KLT.DINV.CD.WD'

question: What is the average value of Adolescent fertility rate in the country whose Alpha2Code is 1A?
evidence: average value = AVG(Value) where IndicatorName = 'Adolescent fertility rate (births per 1,000 women ages 15-19)'
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(T2.Value) AS REAL) / COUNT(T2.Year) FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN Indicators AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Alpha2Code = '1A' AND T2.IndicatorName = 'Adolescent fertility rate (births per 1,000 women ages 15-19)'

question: What is the average occupation of users whose ratings are not more than 2 ?
SQL:
SELECT AVG(CAST(u.`occupation` AS REAL)) AS avg_occupation
FROM `users` u
WHERE u.`userid` IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT `userid`
      FROM `u2base`
      WHERE CAST(`rating` AS REAL) <= 2
);

question: List any 5 movies from other countries which is not English.
evidence: not in English can be represented by isEnglish = 'F'
SQL: SELECT movieid FROM movies WHERE country = 'other' AND isEnglish = 'F';

question: List the id of male users who gave ratings of less than 3 to French movies with running time of 2.
evidence: Male users mean that u_gender = 'M'; France is a country
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.userid FROM u2base AS T1 INNER JOIN movies AS T2 ON T1.movieid = T2.movieid INNER JOIN users AS T3 ON T1.userid = T3.userid WHERE T2.country = 'France' AND T2.runningtime = 2 AND T1.rating < 3 AND T3.u_gender = 'M'

question: How many female actors acted in the movies of year 4?
evidence: Female actors mean that a_gender = 'F'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.actorid) FROM movies AS T1 INNER JOIN movies2actors AS T2 ON T1.movieid = T2.movieid INNER JOIN actors AS T3 ON T2.actorid = T3.actorid WHERE T3.a_gender = 'F' AND T1.year = 4

question: Movies with rating 3 are viewed by audiences in which distinct age group?
evidence: The audience and users are the same meaning
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.age FROM u2base AS T1 INNER JOIN users AS T2 ON T1.userid = T2.userid WHERE T1.rating = 3

question: Among the action movies from the USA, how many of them are not in English?
evidence: USA is a country; not in English can be represented by isEnglish = 'F'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.movieid) FROM movies AS T1 INNER JOIN movies2directors AS T2 ON T1.movieid = T2.movieid WHERE T1.country = 'USA' AND T1.isEnglish = 'F' AND T2.genre = 'Action'

question: Among American movies, please calculate the percentage of movies with negative critical reception.
evidence: negative critical reception' refers to percentage of movies with a rating of 1, which = [count(rating = 1) / count(all movies)] * 100%
SQL:
SELECT 100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN u.`rating` = 1 THEN m.`movieid` END) /
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `movies` WHERE `country` = 'USA') AS neg_crit_pct
FROM `movies` m
INNER JOIN `u2base` u ON u.`movieid` = m.`movieid`
WHERE m.`country` = 'USA';

question: How many female actors have been played a role in any of French or USA movies?
evidence: French and USA are two countries; Female actors mean that a_gender = 'F'
SQL:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT a.actorid)
FROM `actors` AS a
JOIN `movies2actors` AS m2a ON a.actorid = m2a.actorid
JOIN `movies` AS m ON m2a.movieid = m.movieid
WHERE a.a_gender = 'F'
  AND m.country IN ('France', 'USA');

question: How many different actors have played a role in movies that have the highest average rating?
evidence: highest average rating refers to MAX(AVG(rating))
SQL: SELECT SUM(cnt) FROM (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.actorid) cnt FROM u2base AS T1 INNER JOIN movies2actors AS T2 ON T1.movieid = T2.movieid GROUP BY T1.movieid HAVING AVG(T1.rating) = (SELECT AVG(T3.rating) FROM u2base AS T3 INNER JOIN movies2actors AS T4 ON T3.movieid = T4.movieid GROUP BY T3.movieid ORDER BY AVG(T3.rating) DESC LIMIT 1))

question: How many female actresses appeared in the movie 2312852, what country was it in, and what was it's running time?
evidence: female actresses mean that a_gender = 'F'
SQL: SELECT SUM(IIF(T1.a_gender = 'F', 1, 0)) , T3.country, T3.runningtime FROM actors AS T1 INNER JOIN movies2actors AS T2 ON T1.actorid = T2.actorid INNER JOIN movies AS T3 ON T2.movieid = T3.movieid WHERE T2.movieid = 2312852 GROUP BY T3.country, T3.runningtime

question: Please list the names of all the products ordered in order CA-2011-112326 in superstores in the center.
evidence: names of all the products refers to "Product Name"; order CA-2011-112326 refers to "Order ID" = 'CA-2011-112326'; in the center refers to Region = 'Central';
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT P.`Product Name`
FROM `central_superstore` AS CS
JOIN `product` AS P
  ON P.`Product ID` = CS.`Product ID`
 AND P.`Region` = 'Central'  
WHERE CS.`Order ID` = 'CA-2011-112326';

question: How many orders has Aimee Bixby made?
evidence: Aimee Bixby made refers to "Customer Name" = 'Aimee Bixby';
SQL:
WITH all_orders AS (
    SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID`, `Region` FROM `central_superstore`
    UNION ALL
    SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID`, `Region` FROM `east_superstore`
    UNION ALL
    SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID`, `Region` FROM `west_superstore`
    UNION ALL
    SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID`, `Region` FROM `south_superstore`
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT o.`Order ID`) AS total_orders
FROM all_orders o
JOIN people p
  ON o.`Customer ID` = p.`Customer ID`
 AND o.`Region`      = p.`Region`
WHERE p.`Customer Name` = 'Aimee Bixby';

question: Among the customers who have ordered the product "Telescoping Adjustable Floor Lamp", how many of them are consumers?
evidence: "Telescoping Adjustable Floor Lamp" is a "Product Name"; consumers refers to Segment = 'Consumer';
SQL:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.`Customer ID`) AS consumer_cnt
FROM people p
JOIN (
  SELECT `Customer ID`, `Region` FROM west_superstore  WHERE `Product ID` IN (SELECT `Product ID` FROM product WHERE `Product Name` = 'Telescoping Adjustable Floor Lamp')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT `Customer ID`, `Region` FROM east_superstore  WHERE `Product ID` IN (SELECT `Product ID` FROM product WHERE `Product Name` = 'Telescoping Adjustable Floor Lamp')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT `Customer ID`, `Region` FROM south_superstore WHERE `Product ID` IN (SELECT `Product ID` FROM product WHERE `Product Name` = 'Telescoping Adjustable Floor Lamp')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT `Customer ID`, `Region` FROM central_superstore WHERE `Product ID` IN (SELECT `Product ID` FROM product WHERE `Product Name` = 'Telescoping Adjustable Floor Lamp')
) o ON p.`Customer ID` = o.`Customer ID` AND p.`Region` = o.`Region`
WHERE p.`Segment` = 'Consumer';

question: What is the name of the product that has the highest original price?
evidence: has the highest original price refers to MAX(DIVIDE(Sales, SUTRACT(1, discount))); name of the product refers to "Product Name"
SQL:
WITH original_price AS (
  SELECT `Product ID` id, `Region` region, original_price price
  FROM (
    SELECT `Product ID`, `Region`, `Sales` / (1 - `Discount`) AS original_price FROM east_superstore
    UNION ALL
    SELECT `Product ID`, `Region`, `Sales` / (1 - `Discount`) FROM central_superstore
    UNION ALL
    SELECT `Product ID`, `Region`, `Sales` / (1 - `Discount`) FROM west_superstore
    UNION ALL
    SELECT `Product ID`, `Region`, `Sales` / (1 - `Discount`) FROM south_superstore
)) SELECT DISTINCT p.`Product Name`
FROM original_price t
JOIN product p
  ON p.`Product ID` = t.id
 AND p.`Region`     = t.`Region`
WHERE t.price = (SELECT MAX(price) FROM original_price);

question: Name 10 products that were shipped first class from the East region.
evidence: shipped first class refers to "Ship Mode" = 'First Class'; Region = 'East'
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.`Product Name` FROM east_superstore AS T1 INNER JOIN product AS T2 ON T1.`Product ID` = T2.`Product ID` WHERE T1.`Ship Mode` = 'First Class' AND T2.Region = 'East'

question: What category does the item ordered by Katherine Murray on 11/4/2018 in the South region belong to?
evidence: ordered by Katherine Murray refers to "Customer Name" = 'Katherine Murray'; on 11/4/2018 refers to "Order Date" = Date('2018-11-04');
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Category FROM south_superstore AS T1 INNER JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.`Customer ID` = T2.`Customer ID` INNER JOIN product AS T3 ON T3.`Product ID` = T1.`Product ID` WHERE T2.`Customer Name` = 'Katherine Murray' AND T1.`Order Date` = '2018-11-04' AND T2.Region = 'South'

question: Who is the customer from the East region that purchased the order with the highest profit?
evidence: highest profit refers to MAX(profit); Region = 'East'
SQL:
SELECT p.`Customer Name`
FROM (
     SELECT `Order ID`,
            `Customer ID`,
            SUM(`Profit`) AS total_profit
     FROM `east_superstore`
     GROUP BY `Order ID`, `Customer ID`
     ORDER BY total_profit DESC
     LIMIT 1
) o
JOIN `people` p
  ON p.`Customer ID` = o.`Customer ID`
 AND p.`Region` = 'East';

question: Name the customers from the Eastern region who has an order that costs the superstore above 8000.
evidence: cost above 8000 refers to SUTRACT(Sales, Profit) > 80000
SQL:
WITH high_cost_orders AS (
    SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID`
    FROM `east_superstore`
    GROUP BY `Order ID`, `Customer ID`
    HAVING SUM(`Sales` - `Profit`) > 8000
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.`Customer Name`
FROM high_cost_orders h
JOIN `people` p
  ON h.`Customer ID` = p.`Customer ID` AND p.`Region` = 'East';

question: How many orders were made by Maxwell Schwartz in 2015?
evidence: Maxwell Schwartz' is the "Customer Name"; in 2015 refers to strftime('%Y', "Order Date") = '2015';
SQL:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT `Order ID`) AS num_orders
FROM (
  SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID` FROM `east_superstore`  WHERE `Customer ID` = 'MS-17770' AND strftime('%Y',`Order Date`)='2015'
  UNION
  SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID` FROM `west_superstore`  WHERE `Customer ID` = 'MS-17770' AND strftime('%Y',`Order Date`)='2015'
  UNION
  SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID` FROM `central_superstore` WHERE `Customer ID` = 'MS-17770' AND strftime('%Y',`Order Date`)='2015'
  UNION
  SELECT `Order ID`, `Customer ID` FROM `south_superstore`  WHERE `Customer ID` = 'MS-17770' AND strftime('%Y',`Order Date`)='2015'
) t JOIN people ON people.`Customer ID` = t.`Customer ID`

question: List the numbers of orders from the Eastern and Western stores in 2015 in two columns, respectively.
evidence: in 2015 refers to strftime('%Y', "Order Date") = '2015'; Eastern store refers to east_superstore; Western store refers west_superstore;

question: List the products ordered by Matt Abelman from the Western store in 2013.
evidence: ordered by Matt Abelman refers to "Customer Name" = 'Matt Abelman'; in 2013 refers to "Order Date" like '2013%'
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.`Product Name` FROM west_superstore AS T1 INNER JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.`Customer ID` = T2.`Customer ID` INNER JOIN product AS T3 ON T3.`Product ID` = T1.`Product ID` AND T3.Region = T1.Region WHERE T2.`Customer Name` = 'Matt Abelman' AND STRFTIME('%Y', T1.`Order Date`) = '2013'

question: Calculate the total superstore's cost of the orders by Brad Thomas in 2016.
evidence: Brad Thomas is the "Customer Name"; in 2016 refers to strftime('%Y', "Order Date") = '2016'; total cost refers to SUM(SUTRACT(MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(Sales, SUTRACT(1, discount)), Quantity), Profit))
SQL:
WITH all_superstores AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT * FROM `east_superstore`  WHERE strftime('%Y', `Order Date`) = '2016'
    UNION
    SELECT * FROM `west_superstore`  WHERE strftime('%Y', `Order Date`) = '2016'
    UNION
    SELECT * FROM `central_superstore` WHERE strftime('%Y', `Order Date`) = '2016'
    UNION
    SELECT * FROM `south_superstore`  WHERE strftime('%Y', `Order Date`) = '2016'
  ) t
) SELECT SUM((T1.Sales / (1 - T1.Discount)) * T1.Quantity - T1.Profit) AS cost FROM all_superstores AS T1 INNER JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.`Customer ID` = T2.`Customer ID` INNER JOIN product AS T3 ON T1.`Product ID` = T3.`Product ID` AND T1.Region = T3.Region WHERE T1.Region = 'East' AND T2.`Customer Name` = 'Brad Thomas'

question: What is the total quantity that Cindy Stewart order "Lexmark X 9575 Professional All-in-One Color Printer" in the south superstore?
evidence: Lexmark X 9575 Professional All-in-One Color Printer' is the "Product Name"
SQL:
SELECT SUM(s.`Quantity`) AS total_quantity
FROM `south_superstore` AS s
JOIN `people`  p  ON p.`Customer ID` = s.`Customer ID`
                 AND p.`Region`     = s.`Region`
JOIN `product` pr ON pr.`Product ID` = s.`Product ID`
                 AND pr.`Region`    = s.`Region`
WHERE p.`Customer Name` = 'Cindy Stewart'
  AND pr.`Product Name` = 'Lexmark X 9575 Professional All-in-One Color Printer';

question: Calculate the percentage of ordered office supplies products in the central and the south superstore.
evidence: office supplies products refers to Category = 'Office Supplies'; central refers to Region = 'Central'; south superstore refers to Region = 'South'; percentage = divide(SUM(Product ID where Category = 'Office Supplies'), count(Product ID)) * 100 where Region = 'Central' OR Region = 'South'
SQL:
SELECT
  100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN p.`Category` = 'Office Supplies' THEN s.Quantity ELSE 0 END) / SUM(s.Quantity) AS pct_office_supplies
FROM (
      SELECT `Product ID`, `Region`, Quantity FROM `central_superstore`
      UNION ALL
      SELECT `Product ID`, `Region`, Quantity FROM `south_superstore`
) s
JOIN `product` p
  ON p.`Product ID` = s.`Product ID`
 AND p.`Region`     = s.`Region`;

question: How many orders purchased by Aaron Bergman have been delivered with the slowest shipping speed?
evidence: slowest shipping speed refers to "Ship Mode" = 'Standard Class'
SQL:
WITH bergman AS (
    SELECT `Customer ID`
    FROM people
    WHERE `Customer Name` = 'Aaron Bergman'
), all_orders AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT `Order ID`
    FROM central_superstore  WHERE `Customer ID` IN (SELECT `Customer ID` FROM bergman) AND `Ship Mode` = 'Standard Class'
    UNION
    SELECT DISTINCT `Order ID`
    FROM west_superstore     WHERE `Customer ID` IN (SELECT `Customer ID` FROM bergman) AND `Ship Mode` = 'Standard Class'
    UNION
    SELECT DISTINCT `Order ID`
    FROM east_superstore     WHERE `Customer ID` IN (SELECT `Customer ID` FROM bergman) AND `Ship Mode` = 'Standard Class'
    UNION
    SELECT DISTINCT `Order ID`
    FROM south_superstore    WHERE `Customer ID` IN (SELECT `Customer ID` FROM bergman) AND `Ship Mode` = 'Standard Class'
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS orders_with_slowest_shipping
FROM all_orders;

question: What is the highest profit order in the Central superstore of customers from Houston, Texas?
evidence: highest profit refers to max(Profit); Houston, Texas refers to City = 'Houston' and State = 'Texas'
SQL:
WITH order_profit AS (
  SELECT T1.`Order ID`, SUM(T1.`Profit`) AS total_profit
  FROM `central_superstore` AS T1
  JOIN `people` AS T2
    ON T1.`Customer ID` = T2.`Customer ID` AND T2.Region = T1.Region
  WHERE T2.`City`  = 'Houston'
    AND T2.`State` = 'Texas'
  GROUP BY T1.`Order ID`
) SELECT `Order ID`
FROM order_profit
WHERE total_profit = (SELECT MAX(total_profit) FROM order_profit);

question: How many orders were made by Alan Barnes in 2015 at the Central superstore?
evidence: in 2015 refers to "Order Date" = '2015%'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.`Order ID`) FROM people AS T1 INNER JOIN central_superstore AS T2 ON T1.`Customer ID` = T2.`Customer ID` WHERE T1.`Customer Name` = 'Alan Barnes' AND STRFTIME('%Y', T2.`Order Date`) = '2015'

question: Provide the product's name of the product with the highest total sales in the South region.
evidence: highest sales refers to max(SUM(Sales))
SQL:
SELECT p.`Product Name`
FROM south_superstore s
JOIN product p
  ON s.`Product ID` = p.`Product ID` AND p.`Region` = 'South'
GROUP BY s.`Product ID`
HAVING SUM(s.`Sales`) = (
  SELECT SUM(s1.Sales)
  FROM south_superstore s1
  JOIN product p1
    ON s1.`Product ID` = p1.`Product ID` AND p1.`Region` = 'South'
  GROUP BY s1.`Product ID`
  ORDER BY SUM(s1.Sales) DESC LIMIT 1)

question: In which year has the greatest number of cases where Handgun was used as weapon?
evidence: year refers to year(date); the greatest number of cases refers to max(count(case_number)); OS Spray was used as weapon refers to subject_weapon = 'OS Spray'
SQL:
SELECT year
FROM (
    SELECT SUBSTR(`date`,1,4) AS year,
           COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM incidents
    WHERE `subject_weapon` = 'Handgun'
    GROUP BY year
)
WHERE cnt = (
    SELECT MAX(cnt)
    FROM (
        SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
        FROM incidents
        WHERE `subject_weapon` = 'Handgun'
        GROUP BY SUBSTR(`date`,1,4)
    )
);

question: Of all male officers, what percentage are black officers?
evidence: male refers to gender = 'M'; black refers to race = 'B'; percentage = divide(count(officers where race = 'B'), count(officers)) where gender = 'M' * 100%

question: Of the black officers, how many of them investigated cases between the years 2010 and 2015?
evidence: black refers to race = 'B'; between the years 2010 and 2015 refers to date between '2010-01-01' and '2015-12-31'; case refers to case_number
SQL:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.full_name)
FROM officers AS T1
JOIN incidents AS T2 ON T1.case_number = T2.case_number
WHERE T1.race = 'B'
  AND T2.date BETWEEN '2010-01-01' AND '2015-12-31';

question: For the pairs of genes with one having 9 chromosomes and the other having 6 chromosomes, what is the highest expression correlation score?
SQL:
SELECT MAX(I.`Expression_Corr`)
FROM `Interactions` AS I
JOIN `Genes` AS G1 ON G1.`GeneID` = I.`GeneID1`
JOIN `Genes` AS G2 ON G2.`GeneID` = I.`GeneID2`
WHERE (G1.`Chromosome` = '9' AND G2.`Chromosome` = '6')
   OR (G1.`Chromosome` = '6' AND G2.`Chromosome` = '9');

question: Please list the motif of the genes that are located in the cytoplasm and have 7 chromosomes.
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT `Motif`
FROM `Genes`
WHERE `Localization` = 'cytoplasm'
  AND `Chromosome` = 7;

question: What percentage of genes located in the cytoskeleton are of unknown class? And of these, how many are not conditional phenotypes?
evidence: Percentage = count(genes located in the cytoskeleton unknown class) / count(genes located in the cytoskeleton) * 100%; unknown class refers to Class IN ('Unknown','?')
SQL:
WITH cytoskel AS (
    SELECT `GeneID`,
           MAX(CASE WHEN `Class` IN ('Unknown','?') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS is_unknown,
           MAX(CASE WHEN `Phenotype`='Conditional phenotypes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS is_conditional
    FROM `Genes`
    WHERE `Localization`='cytoskeleton'
    GROUP BY `GeneID`
)
SELECT
    100.0 * SUM(is_unknown) / COUNT(*) AS pct_unknown_class,
    SUM(CASE WHEN is_unknown=1 AND is_conditional=0 THEN 1 END) AS not_conditional_unknown
FROM cytoskel;

question: Taking all the essential genes of the transcription factors class located in the nucleus as a reference, list the ids of all the reference genes that carry out a genetic-type interaction with another gene.
SQL:
WITH nuc_ess_tf AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT `GeneID`
  FROM `Genes`
  WHERE `Localization` = 'nucleus'
    AND `Class` = 'Transcription factors'
    AND `Essential` = 'Essential'
)
SELECT DISTINCT e.`GeneID`
FROM nuc_ess_tf e
JOIN `Interactions` i
  ON (i.`GeneID1` = e.`GeneID` OR i.`GeneID2` = e.`GeneID`)
WHERE i.`Type` LIKE 'Genetic%';

question: What is the name and category of the app with the highest amount of -1 sentiment polarity score?
evidence: highest amount of -1 sentiment polarity score refers to MAX(Count(Sentiment_Polarity = 1.0))
SQL:
WITH counts AS (
  SELECT `App`, COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM user_reviews
  WHERE `Sentiment_Polarity` = '-1.0'
  GROUP BY `App`
),
maxcnt AS (
  SELECT MAX(cnt) AS max_cnt FROM counts
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.`App`, p.`Category`
FROM counts c
JOIN maxcnt m ON c.cnt = m.max_cnt
JOIN playstore p ON p.`App` = c.`App`;

question: What is the total sentiment subjectivity of each app that has multiple genres?
evidence: multiple genres refers to COUNT(Genres>1; total sentiment subjectivity = Sum(Sentiment_Subjectivity);
SQL:
SELECT
    t1.`App`,
    SUM(CAST(t2.`Sentiment_Subjectivity` AS REAL)) AS total_sentiment_subjectivity
FROM `playstore` AS t1
JOIN `user_reviews` AS t2 ON t1.`App` = t2.`App`
WHERE t1.`Genres` LIKE '%;%'
  AND t2.`Sentiment_Subjectivity` != 'nan'
GROUP BY t1.`App`;

question: Which free app has the most Negative comments?
evidence: free app refers to Type = 'Free'; negative comment refers to Sentiment = 'Negative'; free app with most negative comments refers to MAX(COUNT(Sentiment = 'Negative')) where Type = 'Free';
SQL:
SELECT u.`App`
FROM `user_reviews` AS u
JOIN (
        SELECT DISTINCT `App`
        FROM `playstore`
        WHERE `Type` = 'Free'
     ) AS p
  ON u.`App` = p.`App`
WHERE u.`Sentiment` = 'Negative'
GROUP BY u.`App`
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the rating for "Garden Coloring Book"? List all of its reviews.
evidence: Golfshot Plus: Golf GPS refers to App = 'Golfshot Plus: Golf GPS'; review refers to Translated_Review;
SQL: SELECT T1.Rating, T2.Translated_Review FROM playstore AS T1 INNER JOIN user_reviews AS T2 ON T1.App = T2.App WHERE T1.App = 'Garden Coloring Book'

question: What percentage of no comment reviews are from "Teen" content rating apps?
evidence: no comment refers to Translated_Review = 'nan'; percentage = DIVIDE((SUM(Content Rating = 'Teen')), COUNT(*)) * 100;
SQL:
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN p.`Content Rating` = 'Teen' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS pct_no_comment_from_teen
FROM `user_reviews` u
JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT `App`, `Content Rating` FROM `playstore`) p
     ON p.`App` = u.`App`
WHERE u.`Translated_Review` = 'nan';

question: What is the average review number for application with 5 rating?
evidence: average review = AVG(Review); application refers to app; Rating = 5;
SQL: SELECT AVG(Reviews) FROM playstore WHERE Rating = 5

question: Among all apps with 4.7 ratings, what is the percentage of application having more positives sentiment than negative sentiment?
evidence: percentage = DIVIDE(COUNT(app that has a 4.7 rating and has more positive sentiment than negative sentiment), COUNT(app that has a 4.7 rating))*100 as percentage; having more positive sentiment than negative sentiment refers to Sentiment = 'Positive'>Sentiment = 'Negative';
SQL:
WITH posneg AS (
  SELECT `App`,
         SUM(CASE WHEN `Sentiment` = 'Positive' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS pos_cnt,
         SUM(CASE WHEN `Sentiment` = 'Negative' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS neg_cnt
  FROM `user_reviews`
  GROUP BY `App`
),
apps AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT `App`
  FROM `playstore`
  WHERE `Rating` = 4.7
)
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN COALESCE(pos_cnt,0) > COALESCE(neg_cnt,0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
       / COUNT(*) AS percentage
FROM apps
LEFT JOIN posneg USING (`App`);

question: How much is the size of Browser 4G and how many users have a pretty positive favorability on it?
evidence: Browser 4G is the App; pretty positive favorability refers to Sentiment_Polarity score >= 0.5
SQL:
SELECT T1.`Size`, COUNT(*) AS positive_cnt
FROM `playstore` AS T1
JOIN `user_reviews` AS T2 ON T1.`App` = T2.`App`
WHERE T1.`App` = 'Browser 4G' AND CAST(T2.`Sentiment_Polarity` AS REAL) >= 0.5
GROUP BY T1.`Size`;

question: How much is the average sentiment polarity score of Golf GPS Rangefinder: Golf Pad and what is it's rating in the Google Play Store?
evidence: average sentiment polarity score = AVG(Sentiment_Polarity); Golf GPS Rangefinder: Golf Pad  is the App;
SQL:
SELECT AVG(CAST(`Sentiment_Polarity` AS REAL))   AS avg_sentiment,
       `Rating`
FROM `playstore`  p
JOIN `user_reviews` u ON p.`App` = u.`App`
WHERE p.`App` = 'Golf GPS Rangefinder: Golf Pad'
  AND u.`Sentiment_Polarity` <> 'nan'
GROUP BY `Rating`;

question: For the Honkai Impact 3rd App, what is the highest sentiment polarity score and what genre does it belong to?
evidence: highest sentiment polarity score refers to MAX(Sentiment_Polarity);
SQL:
SELECT MAX(CAST(ur.`Sentiment_Polarity` AS REAL)) AS max_polarity,
       p.`Genres`
FROM playstore AS p
JOIN user_reviews AS ur ON p.`App` = ur.`App`
WHERE p.`App` = 'Honkai Impact 3rd'
  AND ur.`Sentiment_Polarity` != 'nan'
GROUP BY p.`Genres`;

question: Which education App has the worst rating?
evidence: education App refers to Category = 'EDUCATION'; worst rated app refers to Rating = 1;
SQL:
SELECT `App`
FROM `playstore`
WHERE `Category` = 'EDUCATION'
AND Rating = (SELECT MIN(Rating) FROM playstore WHERE Category = 'EDUCATION' AND Rating IS NOT NULL)

question: List all the customers with name containing the word 'Group'.
evidence: name containing the word 'Group' refers to Customer Names LIKE '%Group%';
SQL: SELECT T FROM ( SELECT IIF(`Customer Names` LIKE '%Group%', `Customer Names`, NULL) AS T FROM Customers ) WHERE T IS NOT NULL

question: List all the order numbers along with its product name for each order under the sales team of 'Douglas Tucker'.
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.OrderNumber, T1.`Product Name` FROM Products AS T1 INNER JOIN `Sales Orders` AS T2 ON T2._ProductID = T1.ProductID INNER JOIN `Sales Team` AS T3 ON T3.SalesTeamID = T2._SalesTeamID WHERE T3.`Sales Team` = 'Douglas Tucker'

question: Among orders in 2020, name the customers who had the greatest discount applied for 'Cocktail Glasses'
evidence: MAX(Discount Applied) where Product Name = 'Cocktail Glasses'; orders in 2020 refer to the OrderDate between 01-01-2020 and 31-12-2020;
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.`Customer Names` FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN `Sales Orders` AS T2 ON T2._CustomerID = T1.CustomerID INNER JOIN Products AS T3 ON T3.ProductID = T2._ProductID WHERE T3.`Product Name` = 'Cocktail Glasses' AND SUBSTR(T2.OrderDate, -2) = '20' AND T2.`Discount Applied` = ( SELECT T2.`Discount Applied` FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN `Sales Orders` AS T2 ON T2._CustomerID = T1.CustomerID INNER JOIN Products AS T3 ON T3.ProductID = T2._ProductID WHERE T3.`Product Name` = 'Cocktail Glasses' AND T2.OrderDate LIKE '%/%/20' ORDER BY T2.`Discount Applied` DESC LIMIT 1 )

question: List the numbers,  the customer name, and the order dates of the orders that has the highest unit cost.
evidence: the highest unit cost refers to MAX(Unit Cost); who refers to Customer Names; when refers to OrderDate;
SQL:
SELECT so.`OrderNumber`,
       c.`Customer Names`,
       so.`OrderDate`
FROM `Sales Orders` AS so
JOIN `Customers` AS c
      ON c.`CustomerID` = so.`_CustomerID`
WHERE CAST(REPLACE(so.`Unit Cost`, ',', '') AS REAL) = (
      SELECT MAX(CAST(REPLACE(`Unit Cost`, ',', '') AS REAL))
      FROM `Sales Orders`);

question: Name the sales team name who had orders with the greatest net profit in 2020.
evidence: net profit can be computed as SUBTRACT(Unit Price, Unit Cost)*Quantity; the greatest net profit in 2020 refers to MAX(net profit) where OrderDate LIKE '%/20';
SQL:
WITH team_profit AS (
  SELECT st.`Sales Team` team, SUM( (REPLACE(so.`Unit Price`, ',', '') -
         REPLACE(so.`Unit Cost`,  ',', '') ) * so.`Order Quantity` ) profit
  FROM `Sales Orders` so
  JOIN `Sales Team` st ON st.`SalesTeamID` = so.`_SalesTeamID`
  WHERE so.`OrderDate` LIKE '%/%/20'
  GROUP BY st.`Sales Team`
) SELECT team FROM team_profit WHERE profit = (SELECT MAX(profit) FROm team_profit)

question: List down the product IDs and names that include the word "Outdoor".
evidence: names that include the word "Outdoor" refer to Product Name LIKE '%Outdoor%';
SQL: SELECT ProductID, T FROM ( SELECT ProductID , CASE  WHEN `Product Name` LIKE '%Outdoor%' THEN `Product Name` ELSE NULL END AS T FROM Products ) WHERE T IS NOT NULL ORDER BY T DESC

question: Describe the ID, city and region of the stores which are in Allen country.

question: Calculate the percentage of order via in-store channel of customer "Medline".
evidence: Medline is the name of the customer; DIVIDE(COUNT(OrderNumber where Sales Channel = 'In-Store' and Customer Names = 'Medline'), COUNT(OrderNumber where Customer Names = 'Medline'))*100 as percentage;
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.`Sales Channel` = 'In-Store' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1._CustomerID) FROM `Sales Orders` AS T1 INNER JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T2.CustomerID = T1._CustomerID WHERE T2.`Customer Names` = 'Medline '

question: Describe the customer names and lasting delivery periods for the product of "Bedroom Furniture" by wholesale channel in 2019.
evidence: delivery period in 2019 means time between placing of an order and the receipt of product and refers to SUBTRACT(DeliveryDate, OrderDate) where SUBSTR(OrderDate, -2 ) = '19'; Sales Channel = 'Wholesale'; Product Name = 'Bedroom Furniture';
SQL:
WITH filtered AS (
    SELECT  C.`Customer Names` AS customer_name,
            date('20' || substr(S.`OrderDate`,-2) || '-' || 
                 substr('00'||substr(S.`OrderDate`,1,instr(S.`OrderDate`, '/')-1),-2) || '-' || 
                 substr('00'||REPLACE(substr(S.`OrderDate`, instr(S.`OrderDate`, '/')+1, 2), '/', ''), -2) )  AS order_iso,
            date('20' || substr(S.`DeliveryDate`,-2) || '-' || 
                 substr('00'||substr(S.`DeliveryDate`,1,instr(S.`DeliveryDate`, '/')-1),-2) || '-' || 
                 substr('00'||REPLACE(substr(S.`DeliveryDate`, instr(S.`DeliveryDate`, '/')+1, 2), '/', ''), -2) ) AS delivery_iso
    FROM `Sales Orders` S
    JOIN Products  P ON P.`ProductID`  = S.`_ProductID`
    JOIN Customers C ON C.`CustomerID` = S.`_CustomerID`
    WHERE S.`Sales Channel` = 'Wholesale'
      AND P.`Product Name` = 'Bedroom Furniture'
      AND substr(S.`OrderDate`,-2) = '19'
)
SELECT customer_name,
       ROUND(julianday(delivery_iso) - julianday(order_iso)) AS delivery_period_days
FROM   filtered
WHERE  delivery_iso IS NOT NULL
ORDER  BY customer_name;

question: Describe the customer names who had at least an order with over 3800 USD in net profit.
evidence: over 3800 USD in net profit refers to SUBTRACT(Unit Price, Unit Cost) * Quantity where Net Profit > 3800;
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT c.`Customer Names`
FROM `Sales Orders` s
JOIN Customers c ON c.`CustomerID` = s.`_CustomerID`
WHERE (CAST(REPLACE(s.`Unit Price`, ',', '') AS REAL) -
       CAST(REPLACE(s.`Unit Cost`,  ',', '') AS REAL)) * s.`Order Quantity` > 3800;

question: Please list the names of customers who have total orders of over 3 in 2018.
evidence: total orders of over 3 in 2018 refer to COUNT(OrderNumber) > 3 where SUBSTR(OrderDate, -2) = '18';
SQL:
SELECT C.`Customer Names`
FROM `Sales Orders` AS S
JOIN Customers AS C ON C.`CustomerID` = S.`_CustomerID`
WHERE substr(S.`OrderDate`, -2) = '18'
GROUP BY S.`_CustomerID`
HAVING COUNT(*) > 3;

question: What is the percentage of total orders of Stephen Payne that had a net profit of over 1000?
evidence: Sales Team = 'Stephen Payne'; net profit can be computed as SUM(SUBTRACT(Unit Price, Unit Cost) * Quantity); DIVIDE(COUNT(OrderNumber where Sales Team = 'Stephen Payne' and Net Profit > 1000)), (COUNT(OrderNumber where Sales Team = 'Stephen Payne')) as percentage;
SQL:
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN total_profit > 1000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT SUM(CAST((REPLACE(T1.`Unit Price`, ',', '') - REPLACE(T1.`Unit Cost`, ',', '')) AS REAL) * T1.`Order Quantity`) total_profit,   
         T1.OrderNumber 
  FROM `Sales Orders` AS T1 
  INNER JOIN `Sales Team` AS T2 
    ON T2.SalesTeamID = T1._SalesTeamID 
  WHERE T2.`Sales Team` = 'Stephen Payne'
  GROUP BY OrderNumber)

question: List the 5 sales teams that have made sales with the highest net profits.
evidence: highest net profit = Max(SUM(Subtract (Unit Price, Unit Cost)*Quantity))
SQL:
SELECT st.`Sales Team`
FROM `Sales Orders` so
JOIN `Sales Team` st ON st.`SalesTeamID` = so.`_SalesTeamID`
GROUP BY st.`SalesTeamID`
ORDER BY SUM((CAST(REPLACE(so.`Unit Price`, ',', '') AS REAL) - CAST(REPLACE(so.`Unit Cost`, ',', '') AS REAL)) * so.`Order Quantity`) DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: To which region does the sales team that has used the WARE-MKL1006 warehouse the most times for its shipments belong?
evidence: "WARE-MKL1006" is the WarehouseCode; most shipment to region refers to Max(Count(Region))
SQL:
SELECT ST.`Region`
FROM (
    SELECT `_SalesTeamID`
    FROM `Sales Orders`
    WHERE `WarehouseCode` = 'WARE-MKL1006'
    GROUP BY `_SalesTeamID`
    ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
    LIMIT 1
) AS top
JOIN `Sales Team` ST
  ON ST.`SalesTeamID` = top.`_SalesTeamID`;

question: At what Latitude and Longitude is the store that has used the WARE-PUJ1005 warehouse the fewest times?
evidence: WARE-PUJ1005 is the WarehouseCode; fewest times refers to Min (Count(WarehouseCode))
SQL:
WITH loc_cnt AS (
  SELECT T2.Latitude, T2.Longitude, COUNT(*) cnt 
  FROM `Sales Orders` AS T1 
  INNER JOIN `Store Locations` AS T2 
    ON T2.StoreID = T1._StoreID 
  WHERE T1.WarehouseCode = 'WARE-PUJ1005' 
  GROUP BY T2.StoreID
) SELECT Latitude, Longitude
FROM loc_cnt
WHERE cnt = (SELECT MIN(cnt) FROM loc_cnt)

question: In the Northeast region, what is the average household income for each city located in the state with the highest number of stores?
evidence: average household income = Divide (Sum(Household Income), Count(City Name)); highest number of store refers to Max(Count(StoreID))
SQL:
SELECT
    sl.`City Name`,
    AVG(sl.`Household Income`) AS avg_household_income
FROM `Store Locations` sl
JOIN `Regions` r ON r.`StateCode` = sl.`StateCode`
WHERE r.`Region` = 'Northeast'
  AND sl.`StateCode` IN (
        SELECT sl2.`StateCode`
        FROM `Store Locations` sl2
        JOIN `Regions` r2 ON r2.`StateCode` = sl2.`StateCode`
        WHERE r2.`Region` = 'Northeast'
        GROUP BY sl2.`StateCode`
        HAVING COUNT(*) = (
          SELECT COUNT(*)
          FROM `Store Locations` sl3
          JOIN `Regions` r3 ON r3.`StateCode` = sl3.`StateCode`
          WHERE r3.`Region` = 'Northeast'
          GROUP BY sl3.`StateCode`
          ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1)
        
  )
GROUP BY sl.`City Name`;

question: What is the household income of Glendale, Arizona?
evidence: "Glendale" is the City Name; Arizona is the State
SQL: SELECT `Household Income` FROM `Store Locations` WHERE `City Name` = 'Glendale' AND State = 'Arizona'

question: From the Spanish LaLiga division in the 2017 season, which team won the most times as a local team, what's percentage of home matches did the team win?
evidence: local team refers to HomeTeam; Spanish means belong to the country = 'Spain'; LaLiga is a name of division; won as a local team refers to FTR = 'H', where H stands for home victory; DIVIDE(COUNT(Div where name = 'LaLiga', country = 'Spain', season = 2017, FRT = 'H'), COUNT(Div where name = 'LaLiga', country = 'Spain', season = 2017)) as percentage;
SQL:
WITH team_stats AS (
  SELECT T1.`HomeTeam`,
         SUM(CASE WHEN T1.`FTR` = 'H' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS home_wins,
         COUNT(*) AS total_home_matches
  FROM `matchs` T1
  JOIN `divisions` T2 ON T1.`Div` = T2.`division`
  WHERE T2.`name` = 'LaLiga'
    AND T2.`country` = 'Spain'
    AND T1.`season` = 2017
  GROUP BY T1.`HomeTeam`
)
SELECT `HomeTeam`,
       home_wins * 100.0 / total_home_matches AS win_percentage
FROM team_stats
ORDER BY home_wins DESC, `HomeTeam`
LIMIT 1;

question: For the Ligue 2 game that made the most goals, who is the winner of that game?
evidence: Ligue 2 is the name of division; the most goals refer to MAX(SUM(FTHG, FTAG)) which are short names for Final-time Home-team Goals and Final-time Away-team Goals;
SQL:
SELECT CASE
         WHEN m.FTR = 'H' THEN m.HomeTeam
         WHEN m.FTR = 'A' THEN m.AwayTeam
         ELSE NULL
       END AS winner
FROM   matchs AS m
JOIN   divisions AS d ON m.Div = d.division
WHERE  d.name = 'Ligue 2'
  AND  (m.FTHG + m.FTAG) = (
        SELECT MAX(FTHG + FTAG)
        FROM   matchs m2
        JOIN   divisions d2 ON m2.Div = d2.division
        WHERE  d2.name = 'Ligue 2');

question: Who is the longest serving coach from year 1970 to 1980. List the coach ID and the team(s) he served.
evidence: 1970 to 1980 refers to year between 1970 and 1980; longest serving coach Max(Count(coachID)); list teams refers to GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT `tmID`)
SQL:
WITH coach_years AS (
  SELECT `coachID`, COUNT(DISTINCT `year`) AS yrs
  FROM `coaches`
  WHERE `year` BETWEEN 1970 AND 1980
  GROUP BY `coachID`
), max_yrs AS (
  SELECT MAX(yrs) AS max_yrs FROM coach_years
)
SELECT c.`coachID`, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT t.`tmID`) AS teams
FROM coach_years c
JOIN max_yrs m ON c.yrs = m.max_yrs
JOIN coaches t ON t.`coachID` = c.`coachID` AND t.`year` BETWEEN 1970 AND 1980
GROUP BY c.`coachID`;

question: List all the coaches with more game lost than won from year 2000-2010. List the coach ID, team name and year.
evidence: from year 2000 to 2010 refers to year between 2000 and 2010; more game lost then won refers to lost > won
SQL:
SELECT c.coachID,
       t.`name` AS team_name,
       c.`year`
FROM   `coaches` c
JOIN   `teams`   t
       ON c.`tmID` = t.`tmID`
      AND c.`year`  = t.`year`
WHERE  c.`year` BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010
  AND  c.`lost` > c.`won`;

question: What is the percentage of player who won "All-Defensive First Team" from 1980 - 2000 is from 'NY'.
evidence: "All-Defensive First Team" is the award; ' NY' is the birthState; 1980 to 2000 refers to year between 1980 and 2000; percentage = Divide (Count(playerID where birthState = 'NY'), Count(playerID)) * 100
SQL:
WITH winners AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT `playerID`
  FROM `awards_players`
  WHERE `award` = 'All-Defensive First Team' AND `year` BETWEEN 1980 AND 2000
),
ny_winners AS (
  SELECT w.`playerID`
  FROM winners w
  JOIN `players` p ON p.`playerID` = w.`playerID`
  WHERE p.`birthState` = 'NY'
)
SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM winners) AS pct_ny_winners;

SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.minutes) FROM players AS T1 INNER JOIN player_allstar AS T2 ON T1.playerID = T2.playerID WHERE T1.birthCity = 'Brooklyn' AND T1.nameNick LIKE '%Superman%'

question: From 1962 to 2011, how many coaches received both NBA and ABA awards?
